context.description: German philosophical prose on consciousness and self-awareness. It argues that people voluntarily limit awareness, describes "dual awareness" as simultaneous attention to outer and inner worlds, and reflects on why such experiences are often forgotten.
normalization_notes: Translated visible German prose into English summary.
Mapped raw category to nonfiction philosophy and subcategory to consciousness essay.

rows.paragraph_text: Awareness is voluntarily restricted like a bandwidth limit, and this self-limitation preserves inner consistency.
rows.text_content: Awareness is voluntarily restricted like a bandwidth limit, and this self-limitation preserves inner consistency.

rows.paragraph_text: Dual awareness can hold both the external situation and the inner reaction at the same time, including speech, body language, feelings, and impressions.
rows.text_content: Dual awareness can hold both the external situation and the inner reaction at the same time, including speech, body language, feelings, and impressions.

rows.paragraph_text: The text says this capacity is common but fleeting, often recognized only in hindsight and quickly forgotten.
rows.text_content: The text says this capacity is common but fleeting, often recognized only in hindsight and quickly forgotten.
